mechanism_narrative: Persistent federal funds rate near zero indicates a structurally accommodative monetary stance that compresses discount rates and supports elevated equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.02 over 2 years, or forward returns during zero-rate periods do not significantly exceed periods with rates above 1% (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.5% signals labor market slack and reduced aggregate demand, which depresses corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE > 5.5% over a 3-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and retail sales indicates robust domestic demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon. The interaction of both series above their respective rolling 12-month averages amplifies the signal.
failure_condition: If forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when both HOUST and RSAFS exceed their thresholds, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 105 index points, it suggests the economy is operating above potential, leading to capacity constraints and cost pressures that compress profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns. This mechanism is most active during the late-cycle expansion phase.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases when INDPRO > 105 during periods identified as late-cycle, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A consistently rising level of retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflects strong consumer spending, which is a primary driver of aggregate demand and corporate profitability, leading to short-term positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return following RSAFS releases is not significantly positive on average (one-sided t-test p>0.05) or the rank IC over 2 years is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.0%, it signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates and reduces the present value of future equity cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS > 2.0% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.01) relative to baseline, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high unemployment and near-zero interest rates indicate a depressed economy with limited policy room, signaling persistent weakness that leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when both conditions hold, or if the interaction term in a regression is not significant at the 5% level over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth above $16 trillion (annualized) indicates a robust economy with strong aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon. This mechanism is most active during sustained expansion regimes.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when GDPC1 exceeds 16000, or if the rank IC over a 2-year rolling window falls below 0.03.
